design_decisions.thmsmith_propose_topic: Continuous-time illness-death separable-effects generator commutator. Pre-anchor: closest published anchors are separable-effects results for competing events and illness-death settings, Aalen-Johansen/Markov multi-state formulas, and longitudinal stochastic-intervention identification; closest banked proposal is eid_policy_commutator_phase_v1, with eid_compositional_reallocation_effect_v1 as a secondary comparator. Our theorem is not those because it is not a g-formula, do-calculus, EIF, or policy-kernel product claim: the focal object is an explicit continuous-time generator commutator certificate. Hand object: states 0=healthy, 1=nonterminal event, 2=death; disease component A=[[-alpha,alpha,0],[0,0,0],[0,0,0]] and death component B=[[-beta,0,beta],[0,-gamma,gamma],[0,0,0]], so [A,B]_{0,2}=alpha*(gamma-beta) and the local difference between exp(t(A+B)) and exp(tA)exp(tB) in death probability is t^2*alpha*(gamma-beta)/2+o(t^2). Non-trivial because the phase threshold alpha*(gamma-beta)=0 is derived from primitive transition hazards, not from support/overlap, g-formula, path derivatives, constrained gradients, or LP/Farkas duality. Reject if this collapses to standard Markov-semigroup algebra, the banked policy commutator, separable-effect definition unfolding, or routine path-specific identification.

banked_reason: Stage -1.1 already-solved rejection: the generator certificate is the standard BCH/Markov semigroup commutator term plus separable-effect definition unfolding, not an unresolved causal-identification gap.
